evals.prompt: Black Friday starts in 18 hours. Our hero SKU has 800 sellable left; we allocated 4000 for the weekend. Meta and Google shopping are still at full budget. What do we do?
evals.expected_output: X threshold logic (20% = 800 is at boundary); table stock vs ads; pre-order copy if crossing X; supplier email template; T-24h checklist elements.
expectations: Defines an explicit inventory threshold X or equivalent and states if current stock is below, at, or above it
Includes a Markdown table with at least four rows linking SKU or group, inventory or allocation %, ads status, recommended action, and owner or placeholder
Provides Pre-order or availability switch copy for PDP and cart or checkout when inventory is below threshold
Includes a complete supplier restock chase email with subject line and body with merge-field style placeholders
Mentions ad pacing, pause, or creative alignment when stock is constrained
References T-24h or final-hours readiness where timing applies

evals.prompt: Inventory is above 50% for all SKUs. We're planning BFCM in two weeks—no numbers yet. Give us a framework.
evals.expected_output: How to set X, table template, pre-order block template, supplier template shell, no fake SKU counts.
expectations: Explains how to choose threshold X without inventing specific false inventory figures
Includes table template for stock vs ads sync
Includes pre-order copy framework or placeholders
Includes supplier email template structure with merge fields

evals.prompt: Rewrite our About Us page for tone only.
evals.expected_output: Brand copy; does not force promo inventory defense deliverables.
expectations: Does not output the full mandatory stock-vs-ads table as the primary deliverable
Does not force supplier chase email and pre-order switch for unrelated About Us request
